title: carex glareosa subsp. marina
intro: Carex glareosa subsp. marina, also known as sea sedge, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the sedge family and is native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of sea sedge include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and even exhibiting antimicrobial properties that can aid in the treatment of infections. Traditionally, sea sedge has been used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, often in combination with other plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
